title: Use public Pirelli strategy-window priors
summary: Pirelli and F1 media publish expected one-stop/two-stop strategies and pit windows before races.
rationale: Pirelli and F1 media publish expected one-stop/two-stop strategies and pit windows before races. These windows summarize track abrasion, compound selection, and simulation assumptions that are not fully visible in the tabular columns.
implementation_hint: Create a small Race-Year prior table with PlanA/PlanB stop count, first-stop lap range, second-stop lap range, and preferred compound transitions. Add distance_to_nearest_strategy_window, inside_plan_a_window, and plan_stop_count_pressure features.
expected_effect: Can sharpen probability peaks at realistic stop windows and reduce diffuse positive probability on unlikely laps.
risk: Published strategies are imperfect and safety cars/weather alter realized stops; keep them as soft features, not hard rules.
